result:
Build green. Reporting.

## Root cause

`crates/lua-vm/src/api.rs:1486-1498` (the `LuaValue::Table` arm of `lua_setmetatable`) was a stub: it computed the metatable, ran the GC barrier, then dropped it on the floor with `let _ = (tbl, mt);` and a `// TODO(port): tbl.set_metatable(mt)` comment. So `setmetatable(t, mt)` returned successfully but never recorded the metatable, leaving `t.metatable()` as `None`. `get_tm_by_obj` then found no `__add`, and `try_bin_tm` raised "attempt to perform arithmetic on a table value".

(The "table expected" panic in the original tail was an earlier run — current head was already past that point with this deeper bug.)

## Fix

Replaced the TODO with the actual call: `tbl.set_metatable(mt);`. `LuaTable` in `crates/lua-types/src/value.rs:167` already exposes interior-mutable `set_metatable(Option<GcRef<LuaTable>>)`, so no type-vocab change was needed.

## State after fix

`cargo build -q -p lua-cli` is clean (warnings only, no errors). I did not re-run the test per the instructions — the mega-loop will re-verify. With the metatable now stored, `__add` lookup in `get_tm_by_obj` will succeed and the program should print `15`. The sibling `LuaValue::UserData` arm has the same stubbed-out pattern but doesn't affect this test (userdata field is not `RefCell`-wrapped, separate concern).
